query: /working-through
help me figure out why my unit tests are flaky. don't just tell me the answer — help me develop the diagnostic skill
I want to actually understand how to debug this myself, not have you fix it. walk me through the reasoning, then let me try
I keep getting these kinds of bugs. give me a framework I can apply, then let me apply it to this one
teach me how to think about this kind of problem. don't solve it for me yet
I want to learn to evaluate trade-offs in system design. take me through it for this specific case but make me do the work
guide me through debugging this React performance issue. I want to develop the muscle, not just have you fix it
ok I'm trying to get better at SQL. help me think through how to write this query without giving me the query
give me the framework, ask me to apply it, then react to my attempt. that's how I learn best
I want to get good at financial-statement reading. work through this earnings report with me — but Socratically
just tell me the answer — why is my code throwing this error?
explain why CAP theorem matters
I'm in a rush, just fix this for me
what's the answer to this math problem?
summarize this article for me
I don't have time to learn, just write me the SQL query
translate this French paragraph
write the code for me, I'll figure out how it works later
I already know how to do this, I just need a reminder of the syntax
what's the difference between functional and object-oriented programming?
